canonical_incident_record.title: ACB system down affecting zone 5 pairing at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet)
canonical_incident_record.incident_description: A high-priority production-impacting malfunction was opened for UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) with subject 'ACB System Down.' Source text states the site reported zone 5 was unable to get a pair. Supporting case notes describe an AMR with low battery under the rack, a replacement attempt, and subsequent behavior where AMRs went to the hospital. Later support updates reported that removing robot 158 from the system corresponded with the system beginning to function again.
canonical_incident_record.customer_operational_impact: Production was seriously impacted. The site reported zone 5 could not get a pair, indicating impaired material-handling operations in that area.
canonical_incident_record.what_resolved_it: Source-supported resolution notes state the site removed robot 158 from the system; the robot was then on charger, and the system was reported to begin functioning again and remain operational during approximately 10 minutes of monitoring. A case summary also states robot 158 was shown as not mapped.
symptoms: Zone 5 unable to get a pair
After an AMR with low battery under the rack was replaced, every AMR reportedly went to the hospital
Zone 5 continued having issues until robot 158 was removed from the system

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Case 00228723 created 3/13/2026 7:36 PM; subject 'ACB System Down'; account 'UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet)'; priority '2 - High: Production seriously impacted'.
Description: zone 5 unable to get a pair; AMR had low battery under the rack; site replaced the AMR; every AMR goes to the hospital.
Case status changed from In Progress to Escalated at 8:30 PM.
Support update states site removed robot 158 from the system, it was on charger, and after removal the system began functioning again and remained operational during about 10 minutes of monitoring.
Case status updated from Escalated to Resolved and case closed at 9:20 PM.
Resolution details state site removed robot 158, which was shown to not be mapped, and the system was reported functional again.
Work-order evidence shows emergency/dispatched maintenance context for Haslet hub and references ACB repair in zone 5, though OCR is low quality.

timeline_events.summary: Site reported ACB system issue with zone 5 unable to get a pair.

timeline_events.summary: Site verified earlier low-battery AMR issue and replacement attempt context.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: They verified they had earlier issues with a low battery AMR... AMRs are going to the hospital lane.

timeline_events.summary: Improper remove/add handling was suggested as a likely issue.
timeline_events.details: A note suggested the robots may not have been removed and added properly, so they were not getting tasks correctly, and that the low battery issue started the problem but was not the current issue.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: I bet they didn't remove and add properly, so it isn't getting a task correctly... The low battery isn't the cause of the current issue, it's just started the problem.

uncertainties: OCR contains minor text corruption.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: There is an easier way... Maybe they leave it out for the rest of the sort.

The resolution details state that after removing robot 158, the ECB system was reported to be functional again.

state_assessment.rationale: This is not a live operational UI but a case text page. The visible text reports a serious production-impacting issue and also says the system was reported functional again after a corrective action. Because the artifact records both outage and reported restoration in narrative form, the most conservative state assessment is recovery_in_progress with low confidence.
visible_indicators: Priority is shown as high with production seriously impacted.
Subject indicates system down.
Resolution text says the system was reported to be functional again.
context_indicators: The page combines symptom description and resolution details in one incident summary.
uncertainties: This is a case summary page, not a direct screenshot of system health indicators.
The text references both 'ACB System Down' and 'ECB system was reported to be functional again'; the naming may reflect inconsistency or OCR/source wording.
The reported recovery is narrative and not independently validated by a live status display in this artifact.

candidate_support_hints.support_reason: The artifact is primarily a case summary and reported resolution note, not a repeatable procedural screenshot or step-by-step operator workflow.

review_uncertainty: The artifact is a rendered text page rather than a native Salesforce UI capture with clearly visible field chrome.
The phrase 'every AMR goes to the hospital' is preserved from source text but may be domain-specific shorthand requiring SME interpretation.
The text references 'ACB System Down' while the resolution mentions 'ECB system'; SME review should confirm whether this is source wording, OCR ambiguity, or a true system-name distinction.
quality_notes: OCR quality is marked usable and aligns closely with the visible text.
The final OCR token 'ee' appears to be stray noise and does not affect the main incident content.
This artifact is strong for incident summary and reported resolution context, but weak for direct technical validation.

cards.short_description: Screenshot of a work order detail page shared in a Teams chat, showing an emergency mechanical breakdown ticket for the Haslet hub.
cards.retrieval_text: Teams chat screenshot from Ethan Crowder showing a work order detail page for a Fort Worth, TX Haslet hub location. The work order page shows P1 emergency priority, dispatched status, company covered yes, estimated service cost 2500 USD, and a problem description referencing UPS conveyor or sorting equipment emergency breakdown with an ACB needing repair in zone 5. Includes vendor assignment details and facility contact information. Useful as action or incident context evidence for dispatched maintenance response.

source_supported_claims.claim: The artifact includes a Teams-style message from Ethan Crowder to an internal audience dated March 17, 2026 at 4:01 PM.
The embedded screenshot shows a 'View Work Order Detail' page.
The work order location is shown as US, TX, Fort Worth.
The building field appears as 'THETH - HASLET HUB'.
The work order shows priority 'P1 - Emergency' and status 'D - Dispatched'.
The screenshot shows 'Company Covered: Yes' and an estimated service cost of 2500.00 USD.
The problem description references conveyor/sorting equipment, emergency breakdown, and an ACB needing repair in zone 5.
The assignment section indicates the type is 'Vendor'.
Artifact OCR and page OCR both align with the screenshot showing a work order for an emergency mechanical issue at the Haslet hub.
Stage 2 classified this artifact as an api_client_screenshot and hinted validation evidence, but the visible image more closely resembles a work order or service management page.

state_assessment.rationale: The screenshot shows an emergency-priority work order with status 'Dispatched' and a problem description indicating an emergency breakdown requiring repair. This supports an active abnormal operational condition, but the screenshot is a work order page rather than direct system health telemetry, so confidence is moderate.

Status shown as 'D - Dispatched'
Problem description references emergency breakdown
Problem description references repair needed in zone 5

uncertainties: The screenshot does not directly show OptiSweep application health or API response state.

candidate_support_hints.support_reason: The artifact documents a specific dispatched maintenance work order but does not clearly show a reusable troubleshooting or recovery procedure.

Several OCR-derived names, numbers, and timestamps are partially garbled and should be SME-verified.
The exact work order ID and vendor name are difficult to confirm from the provided image and OCR.
